title: adoption-production-readiness-separation
outOfScope: Claiming production readiness from installation alone.
Changing adopter-owned CI or review policy.

riskAssessment.reason: Readiness claims affect adopter production gates and must remain evidence-based.

executionDecision.reason: User authorized the approved implementation loop.

preReviewWarnings: Verify installed, calibrated, and production-ready states remain distinct.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Readiness reports distinguish adoption_installed, calibration_complete, and production_ready.
Installation without CI, review, and calibration evidence cannot assert production readiness.
Onboarding and adoption tests cover the state separation.
Installation and first-work-item documentation explain the separate states.
guidelines: Readiness states and evidence requirements are documented.
Production readiness remains fail-closed and evidence-based.

intent.problem: Installation completion and production readiness are currently too easy to conflate.
constraints: Do not mark production ready without calibration, CI, and review evidence.
intent.rationale: Explicit state names prevent adopters from treating a successful install as a production approval.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Calibration complete without CI or review evidence

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
